challenges.title: Truncated Text with Ellipsis
challenges.description: Create a card component that displays a title and description. If the description text is too long, it should be truncated with an ellipsis (...).
requirements: Description that truncates with ellipsis after one line

solution.html:
Card Title

This is a very long description that will be truncated with an ellipsis because it exceeds the available width of the container.
solution.explanation: The paragraph uses white-space: nowrap, overflow: hidden, and text-overflow: ellipsis to truncate long text with an ellipsis.

challenges.title: Scrollable FAQ Section
challenges.description: Create a scrollable FAQ section with a fixed height. Scrollbars should only appear when needed.
requirements: Container with fixed height (300px) and width (400px)
Scrollbars appear only when content overflows
Borders and padding for visual clarity

solution.html:
Q1: What is CSS?

CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets and is used for styling web pages.

Q2: What is overflow?

Overflow controls what happens when content exceeds the container dimensions.

Q3: What is overflow auto vs scroll?

Auto shows scrollbars only when needed; scroll always shows them.

Q4: What is text-overflow ellipsis?

It adds ... to truncated text when combined with overflow hidden and nowrap.

Q5: What is overflow-wrap?

overflow-wrap helps break long words to prevent overflow.
solution.explanation: overflow-y: auto ensures the container shows a vertical scrollbar only when the content height exceeds the container's 300px height.

challenges.title: Responsive Image Gallery with Overflow Handling
challenges.description: Create an image gallery where images are contained within fixed-size cards. Images should not overflow or distort their containers.
requirements: Images should fill the card without overflowing

solution.html:
Image 1
Image 2
Image 3
Image 4
Image 5
Image 6
solution.explanation: overflow: hidden on the card clips the image to the card boundaries. object-fit: cover ensures the image fills the space without distortion. The placeholder divs simulate real images.

challenges.title: Custom Scrollbar Chat Interface
challenges.description: Build a chat message interface with a custom-styled scrollbar. Include both sent and received messages.
requirements: Custom scrollbar styling (different color, rounded, thinner)
At least 8 chat messages alternating left and right
Messages should be styled differently for sent and received

solution.html:
Hey! How are you?
10:30 AM
I'm great, thanks!
10:31 AM
Did you finish the project?
10:32 AM
Almost done! Just need to fix a few things.
10:33 AM
Great! Let me know when you're ready to review.
10:34 AM
Will do! Should be ready by EOD.
10:35 AM
Perfect, no rush!
10:36 AM
Thanks for understanding! 😊
10:37 AM
solution.explanation: The chat container uses overflow-y: auto for scrollability. Custom scrollbar is styled with ::-webkit-scrollbar for Chrome/Safari/Edge and scrollbar-width/color for Firefox.

challenges.title: Overflow-Managed Dashboard Layout
challenges.description: Build a dashboard layout that handles overflow in multiple scenarios: a fixed header, a sidebar with many nav items, a main content area with long text, and a card grid. Use different overflow strategies for each section.
requirements: Sidebar with many navigation items - overflow-y: auto
Main content with a long article - overflow-y: auto
Responsive card grid where cards don't overflow horizontally

solution.html:
Dashboard (Fixed Height, No Overflow)
Dashboard Analytics Users Products Orders Settings Help Reports Invoices Support Tickets Announcements Integrations

Dashboard

Total Users - A Very Long Title That Will Be Truncated

1,234 active users this month.

Revenue

$12,345 this month.

Orders

456 new orders.

Support Tickets

78 open tickets.

Scroll down for more content...

More content here.

Even more content.
solution.explanation: Header has fixed height with flex-shrink: 0. Sidebar uses overflow-y: auto with overscroll-behavior: contain and custom scrollbar. Main content uses overflow-y: auto. Cards use grid with auto-fill to prevent horizontal overflow. Card titles use text-overflow: ellipsis.
